data.mypkg#\u003ccode\u003emypkg\u003c/code\u:
Mojo package
mypkgPackage mypkg provides an example for Modo🧯 API docs.
The package has some re-exported dummy members (optional) as well as a subpkg. The docstring for the package looks like this:
""" Package `example` provides an example for Modo🧯 API docs. The package has some re-exported dummy members (optional) as well as a [.subpkg]. The docstring for the package looks like this: <code block excluded> Exports: - mymodule.MyPair - mymodule.MyIntAlias - subpkg """

data.mypair#\u003ccode\u003eMyPair\u003c/code:
Mojo struct
MyPair @memory_only struct MyPair[T: Intable] A simple example struct.
This struct is re-exported by its parent package, so shows up where the user expects it due to the import path. It has aliases, parameters, fields and methods.
Linking to individual members is as easy as this:
Method: [.MyPair.dump], field: [.MyPair.first]. which gives:
Method: MyPair.dump, field: MyPair.first.

data.subpkg#\u003ccode\u003esubpkg\u003c/code:
Mojo package
subpkgA sub-package.
Exports submodule to include all its members. The docsting looks like this:
""" A sub-package. Exports [.submodule] to include all its members. The docsting looks like this: <code block excluded> Exports: - submodule """

data.submodule#\u003ccode\u003esubmodule\u003:
Mojo module
submoduleModule in subpkg.
This module is re-exported by subpkg, so all its members are included.
